titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.5

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=652 status=live modified=2018-08-06 03:31:15.682716+00 | translation moa=653 status=live modified=2017-07-18 22:43:41.550698+00

div.p:
Alex’ . Dei Gratia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue salutem . Sciatis quod concessi
mus Monachis de Melros’ . vt totam terram suam de Etryk’ . per easdem diuisas que continentur in
carta nostra que de eadem terra eis plenius confecta est  in perpetuum habeant in Liberum forestum .
Quare firmiter prohibemus . ne quis sine eorum licentia in dicta terra secet aut uenetur super nostram plena
riam forisfacturam Decem Librarum . Testibus . Waltero . Olifard . Iustic’ . Laodonie . Rogero auenel
Dauid Marscallo . apud Edinburc’ . Vicesimo Octauo die Maii Anno . Regni . domini Regis Vicesimo
secundo .

Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land [sends] greeting. Know that we have conceded to the monks of Melrose, that they should have their entire land of Ettrick for ever in free forest, by the same boundaries which are more fully specified in our charter which was drawn up for them concerning the same land. Wherefore we strictly forbid that anyone without their persmission should cut wood or hunt in the said land, on pain of our full forfeiture of ten pounds. As witnesses: Walter Oliphant, justiciar of Lothian; Roger Avenel; David the marischal. At Edinburgh on the twenty-eighth day of May in the twenty-second year of the reign of the lord king.
